differentials.name: Retina = light-sensitive (sensory) inner layer Attachment:   firm at anterior margin (= ora serrata) and posteriorly at optic disc

differentials.name: Aqueous humor subdivided by iris into:
differentials.raw: Aqueous humor subdivided by iris into:

context: Ocular globe diameter:  22-25 mm Wall:   composed of 3 layers: (a)  fibrous outermost layer Function:   maintaining shape + pressure of globe (b)  Uvea = pigmented vascular middle layer [uvea, Latin = grape] Attachment:   tethered to sclera by arteries + veins ◊  Most frequent site of intraocular metastases! (c) innermost sensory layer √  various layers of globe are NOT discernable at imaging, especially CT √  sclera may be separated from choroid at high-res MRI √  individually visualized in ocular (choroidal / retinal) detachments Contents:   (a)   anterior segment containing ›  anterior cham
